| [JOS-C01](JOS-C01-fractal-self-similarity.md) | Fractal Self-Similarity | adopt |  | Explain why the same governance patterns repeat at every organizational scale. |

| [JOS-C18](JOS-C18-hub-and-spoke-authority.md) | Hub-and-Spoke Authority | adopt |  | Explain the pattern where parent entities see all children but children see only their own scope. |
| [JOS-C19](JOS-C19-human-oversight-spectrum.md) | Human Oversight Spectrum | adopt |  | Explain the four levels of human involvement in agent-assisted work. |
| [JOS-C28](JOS-C28-autopoiesis.md) | Autopoiesis | adopt | n/a | Explain why self-maintaining systems are more resilient than externally-maintained ones. |
| [JOS-C29](JOS-C29-designed-tension.md) | Designed Tension | adopt | n/a | Explain why intentional opposition between competing concerns IS the governance model, not a flaw in it. |
| [JOS-C30](JOS-C30-multi-perspective-validation.md) | Multi-Perspective Validation | adopt | n/a | Explain why decisions improve when subjected to multiple independent viewpoints before commitment. |

| [JOS-C35](JOS-C35-certainty-driven-autonomy.md) | Certainty-Driven Autonomy | adopt | n/a | Explain why the freedom given to an entity should be driven by task certainty, not by rank or position. |

| [JOS-C37](JOS-C37-governance-overhead.md) | Governance Overhead | adopt | n/a | Explain why governance itself is a cost that must justify its existence through the value it creates. |

| [JOS-C45](JOS-C45-declaration-precedes-governance.md) | Declaration Precedes Governance | adopt |  | Explain the physics of why nothing in a complex system can be governed, discovered, or assembled by another agent until it has been *declared*. |
